_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
| Date | Milestone | Status / What the Workspace Must Have Done |
|------|-----------|---------------------------------------------|
| 13 September 2023 | Regulation (EU) 2023/1805 published in OJ L 234 | In force as of this date |
| 1 January 2025 | First compliance reporting period begins; GHG intensity limit −2% vs 2020 baseline in effect | **In force.** Add FuelEU surcharge field to carrier contracts and invoice audit. Request carrier compliance strategies now. |
| 31 December 2025 | Commission must publish implementing act listing neighbouring container transhipment ports (Article 2(2)) | **Pending.** Monitor OJ for publication. Assess impact on Asia–Europe container routing. |
| 31 December 2025 | First compliance reporting period closes | Carriers begin verification and penalty calculation process. FuelEU surcharge invoices for 2025 will follow in 2026 verification period. |
| 2026 (Q1–Q2, indicative) | Verification period for 2025 reporting year; FuelEU Documents of Compliance issued; penalties assessed | Workspace should have surcharge audit framework operational before carrier invoices arrive. |
| 1 January 2030 | GHG intensity limit tightens to −6% vs 2020 baseline; OPS connection obligation begins for containerships and passenger ships at qualifying EU ports | Review carrier fleet composition and fuel strategy at contract renewal. Begin asking carriers about OPS connection capability at EU ports. |
| 31 December 2029 | All island route temporary exemptions expire (Articles 2(3)–(6)) | No direct workspace action; confirms scope stabilisation. |
| By 2031 | Commission monitoring report on RFNBO market share published | If RFNBO < 1% of maritime bunker fuel, 2% RFNBO subtarget activates from 2034. Note as a watch date for long-term contract rate assumptions. |
| 1 January 2034 | 2% RFNBO subtarget potentially activates (conditional on 2031 report) | **Conditional.** If activated, represents a new mandatory cost layer. Ensure long-term freight agreements include a renegotiation clause triggered by RFNBO subtarget activation. |
| 1 January 2035 | GHG intensity limit tightens to −14.5%; OPS connection obligation extends to ports not covered by AFIR OPS requirement where OPS is installed | First material cost step. Carrier surcharges expected to increase significantly at this interval. |
| 1 January 2040 | GHG intensity limit tightens to −31% | |
| 1 January 2045 | GHG intensity limit tightens to −62% | |
| 1 January 2050 | GHG intensity limit reaches −80% | |

*Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/1805, Articles 2(2), 4, 5(4), 6; OJ L 234, 22.9.2023. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2023/1805/oj/eng*

---
